explanation_used:
1. **Opening statement**: This neuron strongly responds to the lexical token "civil" and its immediate compound phrases (such as "civil rights," "civil war," "civil society," "civil servants," or "civil lawsuit") across a variety of grammatical roles and semantic contexts.

2. **Domain coverage**: The pattern appears in multilingual texts (English, Spanish, Italian, German, Polish) and spans legal, political, social, and historical domains. It activates on discussions of lawsuits, rights movements, armed conflicts, government functions, professional occupations (e.g., civil engineer), and even organizational names (e.g., Protezione Civile). The neuron processes the term both as a standalone adjective ("civil") and within fixed multi-word expressions.

3. **Contrast with low-activation examples**: The low-activation texts lack the word "civil" entirely. They cover disparate topics like sports, entertainment, business, holidays, and general news with no semantic connection to civil matters, legal frameworks, or societal structures denoted by that specific term. The distinction is lexical rather than purely conceptual—the presence of the string "civil" is the primary driver.

4. **Closing interpretation**: This neuron functions as a precise lexical detector for the word "civil" and its direct morphological compounds. It captures surface-level token co-occurrence more than a unified high-level concept, as it fires for "civil" in contexts ranging from human rights to aviation authority without deeper semantic filtering.

(Ames, IA) — Iowa State University researchers say their effort to map out the location and habitat of the rare rusty patched bee In Iowa found them in limited quantity. Teams from I-S-U surveyed 50 sites across Iowa twice in recent months. They only found the rusty patched bumble bee at four locations — in Ames, Brushy Creek State Recreation Area near Fort Dodge, and two locations near Dubuque and Yellow River State Forest. The rusty-patched bumble bee was the first ever bee to be listed as endangered in 2017. Researchers hope the findings could help wildlife managers and land stewards reverse the decline of bee populations and support other pollinators more broadly.

results.actual_text: Washington, D.C. While there, he worked with Martin Luther King and the civil rights movement before becoming a teacher of popular theology in Canada and the U.S.,
results.plain_text: John Shields, a union leader who battled on behalf B.C. public servants against both Socred and NDP premiers, has died.  Shields served four terms as president of the B.C. Government and Service Employees’ Union between 1984 and 1999, following a diverse career in the U.S. and Canada during which he became involved in social justice, theology and the interaction between science and spirituality.  “It’s a sad day for the BCGEU. John was a great friend to me and so many of us at the BCGEU and within the labour movement nationally,” Stephanie Smith, current BCGEU president, said in a statement announcing the death of Shields in Victoria on Friday at age 78.  “He stayed connected with us even after he retired. We’ll miss John’s wisdom and fellowship tremendously.”  Shields was born in New York City and held a masters in theology from St. Paul’s College in Washington, D.C. While there, he worked with Martin Luther King and the civil rights movement before becoming a teacher of popular theology in Canada and the U.S., according to his personal website.  After he moved to B.C. in 1969, his interest in social justice led him to get involved in the labour movement.  In recent years, Shields taught labour relations and negotiations at Vancouver Island University, served as director at Matrix Asset Management and was director of operations for the Land Conservancy of B.C.  He ran a consulting firm and served as chair of the non-profit Centre for Earth and Spirit charity, which strives to “reconnect humans to nature and deepen an earth-based spirituality.”  His memoir, The Priest who left his Religion: In Pursuit of Cosmic Spirituality, was published in 2011.  When Shields left the BCGEU in 1999, he rebuked the NDP government for cutting the public service as part of a “pro-business, anti-union” agenda.  The BCGEU credited Shields with making positive changes during his work there, including his bringing more education and skills development for stewards, decentralizing the handling of member grievances away from union headquarters, creating bargaining councils and forming locals.  The union said his most significant achievement may have been his negotiating pay equity for members in the B.C. public service in 1990, which led sectors such as health, colleges, and community social services to follow suit.  “He was a great man,” Smith said. “We’re sad to lose him. We’ll never forget him.”

results.actual_text: 2 y 3 grados. La dirección del Plan de Protección Civil por Riesgo Volcánico de Canarias mantiene el sem
results.plain_text: El Hierro lleva registrados en un sólo día un total de 96 temblores de tierra, aunque sólo dos de ellos han llegado a ser tan fuertes que hasta la población canaria los ha sentido, según datos de la red de vigilancia volcánica del Instituto Geográfico Nacional.  Los temblores más fuertes se registraron anoche. El primero de ellos, de 4.3 grados, se produjo a las 00:30 horas. Le siguieron otros tres seísmos que superaron de cuatro gradis, aunque sólo el de 4.2 fue sentido a las 05:04 horas. Todos ellos se localizaron en el mar al oeste de Frontera.  Desde entonces se han producido hasta 60 nuevas sacudidas de tierra, de las que 14 han superado los 3 grados de magnitud y el resto ha estado entre los 2 y 3 grados.  La dirección del Plan de Protección Civil por Riesgo Volcánico de Canarias mantiene el semáforo amarillo de riesgo sísmico en la isla y ha limitado la circulación por las carreteras que pueden estar expuestas a desprendimientos de rocas.

results.actual_text: Arika Kane delivers an exhilarating rendition of “Love Makes A Woman,” a song
results.plain_text: Arika Kane delivers an exhilarating rendition of “Love Makes A Woman,” a song originally performed by Barbara Acklin. Released via the BSE Recordings label imprint, on the offering, the international soul singer, and songwriter stuns with a vocally-rich performance that will keep listener’s glued.  “I would hear the original song on Sirius XM’s Soul Town in the car all the time. Barbara Acklin’s performance on it is incredible. Since the very 1st time I heard it I felt drawn to it and connected to it and I knew I wanted to sing it. Its message is powerful and it’s a universal women’s anthem,” says Arika. “It was very important to me to make sure I was able to capture the essence and rawness of her delivery. Barbara Acklin was an outstanding talent in her time and I’m thankful she did this record because it moved me so much that I wanted to re-release it so that it could continue to move others in this time as well.”  The original song was written and produced by Barbara Acklin, William Sanders, Carl Davis, Gerald Sims and Eugene Record. This rendition is written and produced by Arika, Lou Humphrey and Jasmon C Joyner.

results.actual_text: AMN / NEW DELHI UNION Minister of Health and Family Welfare, Dr.
results.plain_text: AMN / NEW DELHI  UNION Minister of Health and Family Welfare, Dr. Mansukh Mandaviya today said that the Government was committed to strengthening surveillance of infectious diseases and outbreak response through the National Centre for Disease Control.  Dr. Mandaviya was speaking at the inauguration and foundation stone laying ceremony of a host of health infrastructure facilities in New Delhi. The Minister inaugurated the upgraded National Centre for Disease Control. He virtually laid the foundation stone for the Regional Branch of National Centre for Disease Control in Assam and six state branches in¬†Haryana, Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, Odisha, West Bengal and Mizoram.  Speaking about the Ayushman Bharat Health Infrastructure Mission, Dr. Mandaviya said that the Modi government is making massive strides in creating a robust healthcare system. He further said that pandemic preparedness and disease surveillance, anchored in strong healthcare systems are crucial to ensure better protection from major disease outbreaks. Focusing on the need for awareness among citizens, the Minister said that development of a country is directly connected to the health and well being of its citizens. Talking about the regional branches, Dr. Mandaviya said that the branches will help in combating diseases with regional variations.  During the event, Dr. Mandaviya also laid the foundation stone for two Biosafety Levels-3 laboratories in Himachal Pradesh and Jharkhand through video conferencing
